explanations.14: This declares a private property that holds the Mink object, which is the browser automation tool that lets you interact with web pages during tests.
explanations.25: This creates a new Mink instance and stores it; Mink acts as a wrapper around the browser driver so you can control it programmatically.
explanations.27: A Session represents one active browser connection; you're creating it with a ChromeDriver, which is the tool that actually controls Chrome.
explanations.28: ChromeDriver is the bridge that talks to a running Chrome browser instance; the URL points to where that Chrome instance is listening for commands.
explanations.44: This throws a PendingException to tell Behat this test step isn't implemented yet—it's a placeholder so the test won't fail, just mark the step as incomplete.
